seoTitle: How to Add a Table in a Brevo Email Campaign
metaDescription: Insert and style a data table in a Brevo email using the Simple Editor or Drag and Drop builder, with clean rows, columns, and mobile-friendly spacing.
intro: Displaying event schedules, product comparisons, or itemized order details cleanly inside an email usually means fighting with misaligned columns or hand-coded HTML tables. Brevo includes a built-in table layout generator in both its Simple Editor and Drag & Drop builder, making structured data grids easy to add without custom code.

steps.heading: Open your content workspace
steps.body: Launch the campaign editor and decide whether you'll build the table in the Rich Text Simple Editor or the modular Drag & Drop builder.

steps.heading: Insert the table element
steps.body: Add the built-in table layout generator to your email body from the available content tools.

steps.heading: Map rows and columns
steps.body: Set the number of rows and columns you need, and merge cells where a larger heading or label spans multiple columns.

steps.heading: Configure formatting details
steps.body: Add row background highlights, clean headers, and padded spacing so the table stays readable at a glance.

steps.heading: Validate mobile responsiveness
steps.body: Preview the table on a mobile layout to confirm columns adapt properly to smaller screens rather than breaking or overflowing.

faq.question: Can I build a table without knowing HTML?
faq.answer: Yes, Brevo's built-in table generator in both editors handles the layout without requiring you to code raw HTML tables.

faq.question: Does the table adapt to mobile screens automatically?
faq.answer: Brevo's content engine adapts tabular spacing across desktop and smartphone viewports, though it's still worth previewing before sending.

faq.question: Can I merge cells in the table?
faq.answer: Yes, merging cells is supported for cases like spanning headers across multiple columns.

faq.question: Which editor should I use to add a table, Simple Editor or Drag & Drop?
faq.answer: Both support table matrices, so the choice depends on whether you prefer the simpler rich text interface or the more modular drag-and-drop layout.
